label: Adopt visual hierarchy sub-probe into audit critique hardening
minimalEdit: Add entry point, eye flow, weight, and emphasis as named visual hierarchy probes beneath cognitive load.

label: Adopt affordance and action discoverability probe
minimalEdit: Add clickability, state visibility, CTA clarity, hover-only action, and destructive-action visibility checks.

label: Strengthen cognitive load with priority and scanability checks
minimalEdit: Add task count, buried critical content, aligned labels/data/status, and earned progressive-disclosure bullets.

label: Add compact composition scan to audit polish checks
minimalEdit: Add balance, macro/micro whitespace, rhythm, and gestalt grouping as craft-risk probes.

label: Add optional brand evidence guard
minimalEdit: When mood, voice, or token docs are supplied, cite them; when absent, label brand alignment not assessed instead of inventing rules.

label: Tighten color contrast evidence requirements
minimalEdit: Require contrast findings to include measured pair, actual ratio, minimum ratio, and whether color is the sole state signal.

label: Expand typography polish into named audit probes
minimalEdit: Name scale usage, readability, consistency, and token compliance under typography polish, then route system fixes to foundations.

label: Audit reporting contract is already stronger than visual-critique per-dimension ratings
detail: Keep P0-P3 findings and the five-dimension /20 score; do not adopt pass/minor/major ratings.

label: Token drift and hardcoded value checks are already covered
detail: Visual-critique token compliance adds less than the existing theming/token drift section except for optional brand evidence inputs.

label: AI-tell detection is already stronger in audit than visual-critique
detail: The model-specific tell catalog has concrete checks, owners, and severity rules; visual-critique should not replace it.

direction: Add seven visual-critique dimensions as top-level audit scoring dimensions
reason: Audit already has a five-dimension /20 score; importing seven more dimensions would fragment report semantics.

direction: Require mood.md, voice.md, and tokens.md for every audit
reason: Useful only as optional evidence when supplied; missing brand docs should become not-assessed, not invented policy.

direction: Make audit apply token fixes or exhaustive remediation
reason: Audit files evidence-backed findings and maps owners; foundations and sk-code own accepted implementation changes.

direction: Treat visual hierarchy ratio thresholds as hard release gates
reason: The corpus contains useful calibration numbers, but audit should severity-rank observed user impact unless an accessibility threshold is involved.
